proof: function[T0: LinearOrder](x0: T0 -> Bool) { exists(k0: T0) { is_upper_bound[T0](x0, k0) } = is_bounded_above[T0](x0) }[L](p)
not exists(k0: L) { is_upper_bound(p, k0) }
function(x0: L) { not is_upper_bound(p, x0) }(b)

goal: exists(k0: L, k1: L) { k0 = a and k1 = b and is_bounded_by_interval(p, k0, k1) }

proof: function[T0: LinearOrder](x0: T0 -> Bool) { exists(k0: T0, k1: T0) { is_bounded_by_interval[T0](x0, k0, k1) } = is_bounded[T0](x0) }[L](p)
not exists(k0: L, k1: L) { is_bounded_by_interval(p, k0, k1) }
function(x0: L) { not exists(k0: L) { is_bounded_by_interval(p, x0, k0) } }(a)
function(x0: L, x1: L) { not is_bounded_by_interval(p, x0, x1) }(a, b)

goal: exists(k0: L, k1: L) { is_bounded_by_interval(p, k0, k1) }
proof: function[T0: LinearOrder](x0: T0 -> Bool) { exists(k0: T0, k1: T0) { is_bounded_by_interval[T0](x0, k0, k1) } = is_bounded[T0](x0) }[L](p)

goal: exists(k0: L, k1: L) { is_bounded_by_interval(p, k0, k1) }
proof: function[T0: LinearOrder](x0: T0 -> Bool) { exists(k0: T0, k1: T0) { is_bounded_by_interval[T0](x0, k0, k1) } = is_bounded[T0](x0) }[L](p)
